title: heptapleurum maduraiense
intro: Heptapleurum maduraiense, also known as the Madurai tree fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It is a type of tree fern that is native to the tropical forests of India and Sri Lanka. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. The leaves and roots of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and cold, and are also valued for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
